Assembly: The assembly ilAcaAtra2.hap1.1 is based on 32x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. This species appears to be ZO.

Assembly: The assembly ioArcGran1.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified by its half PacBio read coverage relative to the autosomes and confirmed via synteny analysis with?Hetaerina titia?(GCA_037158775.1).cThe exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (19,695 - 25,152 & 109,206 - 127,429 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (5,615 - 6,187 & 92,561 - 93,593 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly of Illex coindetii (xcIllCoin1) is based on 40X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 24 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 5.3 Mb (with the largest being 3.9Mb). Additionally, 1,395 regions totaling 286 Mb (with the largest being 2.7 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptgaul. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 4 haplotypic regions were removed,totaling 3.9 Mb (with the largest being 1.6 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. The chromosome X has been assigned thanks to its coverage in PacBio reads divided by 2.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Assembly: On Nov 19, 2025 this sequence version replaced LS420036.1.; The assembly fAstCal1.5 is based on ~48x PacBio RSII data, ~112x Illumina HiSeqX data generated from a 10X Genomics Chromium library, and single-enzyme BioNano Irys data. Hi-C was obtained from sperm of an A. calliptera individual from 'Mbenji Island? in Lake Malawi. An initial PacBio assembly was made using Falcon-unzip and the primary contigs extended by merging with a miniasm assembly. The contigs were then scaffolded using the 10X data with scaff10x. After using the PacBio data to gap fill with PBJelly and polish with Quiver, the assembly was manually curated using gEVAL to correct mis-joins and improve concordance with the BioNano data. The assembly was then polished again using the 10X Illumina data and freebayes. Two genetic maps [PMID: 23622422, 25156298] were used to allocate scaffolds to chromosomes, followed by further curation and verification using gEVAL. The Hi-C data was used for manual curation using Pretext View. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP.

The assembly of Linckia multifora (eaLinMult1) is based on 78X PacBio data and 95X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 10 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.719 Mb (with the largest being 0.208 Mb). Additionally, 172 regions totaling 17.404 Mb (with the largest being 1.657 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic regions and 7 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 0.25Mb and 0.36Mb, respectively (with the largest being 0.25Mb and 0.08Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ihStrApic1.hap1.1 is based on 31x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X was identified by alignment to Allygus modestus (GCA_963675035.1). Both haplotypes have been submitted for this genome. A haplotype-specific fission was observed in chromosome 1 of haplotype 2 resulting in chromosomes 1A and 1B for the hap2 assembly. Haplotypic inversions are present in haplotype 1, on gchromosome 1 from ~39.33-57.80Mb and from ~179.89-187.20Mb, chromosome 2 from ~78.78-84.42Mb, chromosome 4 from ~14.21-49.27Mb, chromosome 7 from ~47.23.85.53Mb. Haplotypic inversions are present in haplotype 2, on chromosome 1A ~42.00-59.73Mb, chromosome 1B from ~0.3-37.96Mb, chromosome 2 from ~75.24-80.05Mb, chromosome 4 from ~14.85-50.93Mb, chromosome 7 from ~48.73-89.22Mb. Order and orientation of scaffolds in hap1 chromosome 3 from ~4.68-13.0Mb and chromosome X from ~65.95-68.83Mb, and hap2 chromosome 3 from ~4.76-12.62Mb and chromosome X from ~25.16-28.38Mb is unsure.

Assembly: On Aug 2, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ222375.1.; Genome assembly of Naticarius stercusmuscarum. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Naticarius stercusmuscarum (xgNatSter1) is based on 34X ONT data and 104X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 165 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 5.725 Mb (with the largest being 2.391 Mb). Additionally, 2770 regions totaling 97.68 Mb (with the largest being 5.663 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, no regions were tagged as allelic duplications or contaminants. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 4gg (21,007 - 22,100 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly icCatNigr1.hap2.1 is based on 102x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. The X chromosome was identified by read coverage, no Y chromosome was found. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (1.7 - 5.8 Mbp) and chromosome 4 (8.8 - 9.6 Mbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 8 (8.1-8.5 Mbp).

Assembly: This project presents the alternate haplotype of the diploid genome assembly of the garden speedwell, Veronica longifolia, from a sample provided by Thomas Marcussen. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The daVerLong1.1 assembly is based on 32x PacBio HiFi and 60x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly ihBerCras1.hap1.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. We can see that the two haplotypes on Chromosome 11 show some inversions and rearrangements, being quite divergent. The region on Chromosome 6 from start to 26.4 Mbp are highly repetitive and the order and orientation may be uncertain. The X chromosome was identified based on synteny to Berytinus montivagus.

The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: ONT duplex reads assembled with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named according to standard Malawi cichlid nomenclature.

Assembly: The assembly daArnMont1.hap1.1 is based on 65x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Heterozygous structural variation was observed in the following regions: chromosome 1, 34.25 Mb to 62.8 Mb; chromosome 4, 25.6 Mb to 29.7 Mb; chromosome 6, 28.8 Mb to 46.3 Mb; chromosome 18; 10.4 Mb to 12.4 Mb and chromosome 19, 14.3 Mb to 21.9 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly fMasArm1.3 is based on 52x PacBio Sequel data, 99x coverage Illumina HiSeqX data from a 10X Genomics Chromium library generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, as well as two-enzyme BioNano Saphyr data generated at the Rockefeller University Vertebrate Genome Laboratory and 245x coverage HiSeqX data from a Hi-C library prepared by Arima Genomics. An initial PacBio assembly was made using Falcon-unzip and the primary contigs extended by merging with a miniasm assembly. The contigs were then scaffolded using the 10X data with scaff10x, and using synteny with Lates calcarifer (Asian seabass). After using the PacBio data to gap fill with PBJelly and polish with Arrow, the assembly was polished again using the 10X Illumina data and freebayes. Finally, the assembly was further manually curated using gEVAL to correct mis-joins and improve concordance with the BioNano and Arima Hi-C data. This was the fMasArm1.1 assembly. A updated fMasArm1.2 assembly has been further curated using the Hi-C data to separate out haplotigs, identify further breaks and joins and produce chromosomal-level scaffolds. The further updated fMasArm1.3 assembly includes a mitochondrial assembly produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Chromosomes have been named by synteny to medaka.

Assembly: On Jul 23, 2024 this sequence version replaced OY755026.1.; The fNeoMul1.2 assembly is based on 49x PacBio Sequel data together with 66x coverage 10X Genomics linked read library from one male individual. It was assembled using Falcon-unzip, followed by removal of haplotypic duplication with purge_dups. The assembled contigs were polished using the long reads with Arrow, followed by two rounds of polishing with the linked reads with freebayes and bcftools consensus. The scaffolds were placed onto chromosomes with YaHs (https://doi.org/10.1101/2022.06.09.495093) using 163x coverage Hi-C data. The chromosomes were manually ordered and oriented to match the chromosome order and orientation of the A. calliptera genome (fAstCal1.3; GCA_900246225.5). Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL.

Assembly: The assembly ihGerLacu2.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Gerris lacustris has previously been reported to have XO sex determination~(https://doi.org/10.1080/01650424.2016.1167222). PacBio and HiC data were derived from different unsexed samples. Based on coverage, the PacBio sample is likely male (half coverage X chromosome) and the HIC sample is likely female (no drop in HIC intensity on the X chromosome). scaffold_12, scaffold_16, scaffold_17 and scaffold_18 have half coverage based on PacBio read mapping and lack HiC signal. As such, they could either be derived from a Y chromosome or represent X chromosome structural variation between the samples used for PacBio and HiC sequencing.

Assembly: The assembly drMalDome5.3 is based on 25x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Malus domestica (apple) GCA_004115385.1. From the Hi-C data, inversions between haplotypes can be seen on chromosome 4 11.13-14.86mb, chromosome 6 13.34-17.21mb, chromosome 11 22.9-24.24mb and 30.06-32.57mb, chromosome 12 10.9-11.98mb, chromosome 13 34-37mb.

Assembly: The assembly ilNocFimb1.1 is based on 20x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X identified by identification of ancestral X BUSCO genesThere is an inversion between haplotypes on Chromosome 11 between approximately 2.01-5.33Mb.

Assembly: On Dec 3, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ311864.1.; The assembly of Steromphala pennanti (xgStePenn6.2) is based on 71X PacBio data and 162X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 65 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 3.59 Mb (with the largest being 0.372 Mb). Additionally, 224 regions totaling 38.83 Mb (with the largest being 6.07 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 18 haplotypic regions totaling 14.86 Mb were removed (with the largest being 3.3 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Linckia guildingi (eaLinGuil1) is based on 48X PacBio data and 137X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.148 Mb (with the largest being 0.046 Mb). Additionally, 117 regions totaling 10.792 Mb (with the largest being 0.905 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 16 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 4.51 Mb (with the largest being 0.83 Mb). Chromosome 2 contains a large region from 18 Mb to 30 Mb that appears to be a heterozygous deletion; indeed, both the depth of coverage and the density of Hi-C contacts are low in this interval. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
